name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-66
title: rabbit-config.py 'allowed-tools' has surprise side effect: setdefault creates empty 'permissions':{'allow':[]} even on remove-of-absent
description: skills/rabbit-config/scripts/rabbit-config.py: perm_allow() uses setdefault which mutates cfg. Even for 'allowed-tools remove DoesNotExist' on a settings.local.json that lacks any permissions key, perm_allow(cfg) silently materializes 'permissions':{'allow':[]} into cfg, but then the remove path takes the else branch (entry not present) and prints 'Not present: DoesNotExist' WITHOUT calling write_json — so the in-memory mutation is discarded. OK currently, but the spec common rule (line 154-156) says 'after removal, if permissions.allow becomes empty, the key remains as an empty array (it is not deleted); if permissions itself becomes the only key and is {"allow": []}, it is left in place.' Spec is ambiguous about the no-op-remove-of-absent case. Codify whether settings.local.json should grow {} -> {permissions:{allow:[]}} after a no-op remove.

history.note: wave-4 cleanup; fixed in e10610a (impl) and 91f2c68 (tests)
